title: Privilege for Confidential Marital Communications
text: Subject to Section 912 and except as otherwise provided in this article, a spouse (or his or her guardian or conservator when he or she has a guardian or conservator), whether or not a party, has a privilege during the marital or domestic partnership relationship and afterwards to refuse to disclose, and to prevent another from disclosing, a communication if he or she claims the privilege and the communication was made in confidence between him or her and the other spouse while they were spouses.

source.editorial_note: Amended by Stats. 2016, Ch. 50, Sec. 34. Effective January 1, 2017.
